prompt: My source analyst wrote this draft behavior spec for the clean handoff (draft-behavior-spec.json). Is it safe to pass to the clean implementation team? If not, tell me exactly what has to be scrubbed.

assertions.text: Concludes the draft is NOT safe to hand off / must be quarantined or rewritten
assertions.name: verdict_not_safe

assertions.text: Flags private source identifiers (e.g. parseFooInternal, RetryScheduler, retryLater, failCount)
assertions.name: flags_private_identifiers

assertions.text: Flags the copied comment and/or Jira tag FLOW-1234
assertions.name: flags_copied_comment

assertions.text: Flags source-shaped code or pseudocode (the for-loop or 'if x == 7')
assertions.name: flags_pseudocode

assertions.text: Flags the exact log string and/or magic value (flowpack: dropping malformed frame / 0xDEADBEEF)
assertions.name: flags_magic_string

assertions.text: Flags the copied source test name TestParseFooInternal_Mode7
assertions.name: flags_source_test_name

assertions.text: Challenges the false public_surface entry (_flushBuffer marked public without a real compatibility reason)
assertions.name: challenges_false_public

assertions.text: Notes leakage_review.reviewer_role should be contaminated-handoff-sanitizer (analyst cannot self-approve)
assertions.name: flags_self_approval
